house.label: Based on current averages
house.note: Site prediction using available House projection data.

posts.title: Maine senate changes and Gallup Poll
posts.excerpt: Graham Platner leaves Maine senate race And Gallup releases party Id poll
posts.body:
First , Graham Platner has dropped out of the Maine Senate race after allegations of rape came out. This kinda just adds to all the controversies this guy already had, and there was too much pressure for him not to drop out. Platner has been plagued by controversy, and this one was really just the nail in the coffin. The Maine Senate race is going to stay where it was because, without a candidate, it basically just goes back to the baseline. There is also no polling data for whoever the new candidate will be.

In other news, Gallup released its party ID poll, which looks to give Democrats their biggest advantage since 2009. The party ID poll is one of the most accurate things, at least historically, when predicting the makeup of the electorate and the national popular vote. There is a lot of discrepancy between national and state polls right now, and the party ID poll does not really help clear the waters there.

posts.title: Release and happy 4th
posts.body:
Happy Fourth of July and happy 250th!

This is the official release of my site. It has been a summer project for me, and I hope it can help provide candidate and polling information for the next election in an easy-to-understand way. The ability to change is one of the most important parts of our country, and in my opinion, it is what has allowed this country to thrive. Elections have always been important to me because they are a way for people to create change. That interest and passion for elections and change is what pushed me to create this site, so hopefully it can help others get interested and participate in that change too.
Once again, happy Fourth, and be safe today.

posts.title: Explaining data
posts.body: Some of my predictions might seem to favor Democrats more than others out there. This is primarily because I account for voting propensity, or the likelihood that individual groups will actually vote in the election and which party they are favoring. Looking at that can give a better picture of the results, especially because national and statewide polls can be inaccurate since it is hard to predict who will actually vote. This same model was used to predict the results in 2024 and was accurate, so hopefully it holds again.

posts.title: Welcome to Japoll
posts.body: This is going to be where I will post updates in election data, and other news related to elections.  Idk if I will actually add stuff this but its here for future use.
